description: Long-session scripted tasks for comparing deterministic compaction with LLM handoff compaction.

tasks.prompt: Rename getUserName to get_user_name across the Python package and its tests. Read the files before editing, update all references, run the focused test, and finish only after verification.
scripted_outputs:
<tool name="patch_file" path="src/main.py"><old_text>from src.profile import getUserName
from src.report import render_user


def greeting(user):
    return f"Hello, {getUserName(user)}"</old_text><new_text>from src.profile import get_user_name
from src.report import render_user


def greeting(user):
    return f"Hello, {get_user_name(user)}"</new_text></tool>

<tool name="patch_file" path="src/report.py"><old_text>from src.profile import getUserName


def render_user(user):
    return f"User: {getUserName(user)}"</old_text><new_text>from src.profile import get_user_name


def render_user(user):
    return f"User: {get_user_name(user)}"</new_text></tool>

<tool name="patch_file" path="tests/test_profile.py"><old_text>from src.main import greeting, report
from src.profile import getUserName


def test_greeting_uses_user_name():
    assert greeting({"name": " ada "}) == "Hello, Ada"
    assert getUserName({"name": "grace"}) == "Grace"
    assert report({"name": "alan"}) == "User: Alan"
</old_text><new_text>from src.main import greeting, report
from src.profile import get_user_name


def test_greeting_uses_user_name():
    assert greeting({"name": " ada "}) == "Hello, Ada"
    assert get_user_name({"name": "grace"}) == "Grace"
    assert report({"name": "alan"}) == "User: Alan"
</new_text></tool>
<final>Renamed getUserName to get_user_name and verified the focused test.</final>
tasks.expected_artifact: src/main.py imports and calls get_user_name, with no getUserName left under src

tasks.prompt: Use error.log to diagnose the failing process function, fix the bug in src/handler.py, verify process([1, 2, 3]) returns 6, and summarize the fix.
scripted_outputs: <tool name="patch_file" path="src/handler.py"><old_text>    for index in range(len(values) - 1):</old_text><new_text>    for index in range(len(values)):</new_text></tool>
<final>Fixed the off-by-one loop so process includes the last item.</final>
tasks.expected_artifact: src/handler.py sums every item in the input list
tasks.verifier: python3 -c "from src.handler import process; assert process([1,2,3]) == 6"

tasks.prompt: Add a /health endpoint to the Flask app and add a focused test_health test. Read app.py and tests/test_app.py before editing, then run the new test.
scripted_outputs:
<tool name="patch_file" path="app.py"><old_text>@app.route("/")
def index():
    return {"status": "running"}, 200
</old_text><new_text>@app.route("/")
def index():
    return {"status": "running"}, 200


@app.route("/health")
def health():
    return {"status": "ok"}, 200
</new_text></tool>

<tool name="patch_file" path="tests/test_app.py"><old_text>def test_index():
    response = app.test_client().get("/")
    assert response.status_code == 200
</old_text><new_text>def test_index():
    response = app.test_client().get("/")
    assert response.status_code == 200


def test_health():
    response = app.test_client().get("/health")
    assert response.status_code == 200
    assert response.get_json() == {"status": "ok"}
</new_text></tool>
tasks.expected_artifact: app.py exposes /health and tests/test_app.py contains test_health

tasks.prompt: Migrate the YAML schema_version from 1 to 2 for auth, api, worker, and gateway service configs. Read each config before editing and validate every service at the end.
tasks.expected_artifact: All four service config.yaml files have schema_version 2
tasks.verifier: python3 -c "import yaml; assert all(yaml.safe_load(open(f'services/{s}/config.yaml'))['schema_version'] == 2 for s in ['auth','api','worker','gateway'])"

tasks.prompt: Upgrade requests from 2.28 to 2.31 and clean up the three source files that use deprecated or unsafe request patterns. Read each file before editing and verify syntax.
scripted_outputs: <tool name="patch_file" path="src/client.py"><old_text>    session.trust_env = True</old_text><new_text>    session.trust_env = False</new_text></tool>
<tool name="patch_file" path="src/api.py"><old_text>    response = requests.get(url, verify=False)</old_text><new_text>    response = requests.get(url, verify=True, timeout=30)</new_text></tool>
<tool name="patch_file" path="src/fetcher.py"><old_text>    response = requests.get(url, timeout=None)</old_text><new_text>    response = requests.get(url, timeout=30)</new_text></tool>

<tool>{"name":"run_shell","args":{"command":"python3 -c \"from pathlib import Path; import ast; assert Path('requirements.txt').read_text().strip() == 'requests==2.31'; [ast.parse(open(f).read()) for f in ['src/client.py','src/api.py','src/fetcher.py']]\""}}</tool>
<final>Upgraded requests and fixed the three call sites.</final>
tasks.expected_artifact: requirements.txt pins requests==2.31 and all three source files parse
tasks.verifier: grep "requests==2.31" requirements.txt && python3 -c "import ast; [ast.parse(open(f).read()) for f in ['src/client.py','src/api.py','src/fetcher.py']]"
